en: Better is a poor person who walks in his integrity than one who is crooked in speech and is a fool.
zh: 行為完全的窮人， 勝過說話欺詐的愚昧人。

en: Desire without knowledge is not good, and whoever makes haste with his feet misses his way.
zh: 一個人沒有知識是不好的， 腳步匆忙的，難免失足。

en: When a man's folly brings his way to ruin, his heart rages against the LORD.
zh: 人的愚妄毀滅自己的道路； 他的心卻惱怒耶和華。

en: Wealth brings many new friends, but a poor man is deserted by his friend.
zh: 財富使朋友增多， 但窮人連他的一個朋友也與他分離。

en: A false witness will not go unpunished, and he who breathes out lies will not escape.
zh: 作假見證的必難免受懲罰； 吐出謊言的必不能逃脫。

en: Many seek the favor of a generous man, and everyone is a friend to a man who gives gifts.
zh: 尊貴的人，很多人求他的情面； 慷慨施贈的，人人都作他的朋友。

en: All a poor man's brothers hate him; how much more do his friends go far from him! He pursues them with words, but does not have them.
zh: 貧窮人的兄弟都恨他； 他的朋友更疏遠他。 雖然他多多懇求他們，他們卻不理會。

en: Whoever gets sense loves his own soul; he who keeps understanding will discover good.
zh: 得著智慧的，愛惜自己的性命； 保持明達的，必得益處。

en: A false witness will not go unpunished, and he who breathes out lies will perish.
zh: 作假見證的必難免受懲罰； 吐出謊言的終必滅亡。

en: It is not fitting for a fool to live in luxury, much less for a slave to rule over princes.
zh: 愚昧人生活奢侈是不相稱的， 何況奴僕管轄領袖呢！

en: Good sense makes one slow to anger, and it is his glory to overlook an offense.
zh: 人的明慧使他不輕易動怒； 寬恕別人過失的，是自己的榮耀。

en: A king's wrath is like the growling of a lion, but his favor is like dew on the grass.
zh: 王的忿怒，好像獅子的吼叫， 他的恩寵，如同草上的朝露。

en: A foolish son is ruin to his father, and a wife's quarreling is a continual dripping of rain.
zh: 愚昧的兒子是父親的禍患， 吵鬧的妻子好像雨水不停地滴漏。

en: House and wealth are inherited from fathers, but a prudent wife is from the LORD.
zh: 房屋與財富是祖宗遺留的產業； 唯有明慧的妻子是耶和華所賜的。

en: Slothfulness casts into a deep sleep, and an idle person will suffer hunger.
zh: 懶惰使人沉睡， 閒懶的人必受飢餓。

en: Whoever keeps the commandment keeps his life; he who despises his ways will die.
zh: 謹守誡命的，保全自己的性命； 輕忽自己道路的，必致死亡。

en: Whoever is generous to the poor lends to the LORD, and he will repay him for his deed.
zh: 恩待窮人的，等於借錢給耶和華； 他所行的，耶和華必償還。

en: Discipline your son, for there is hope; do not set your heart on putting him to death.
zh: 趁著還有指望的時候，要管教你的兒子； 不可存心使他死亡。

en: A man of great wrath will pay the penalty, for if you deliver him, you will only have to do it again.
zh: 常發烈怒的人，必須受罰， 如果你幫助他，就必須一而再幫助他。

en: Listen to advice and accept instruction, that you may gain wisdom in the future.
zh: 你要聽勸告，受管教， 好使你將來作個有智慧的人。

en: Many are the plans in the mind of a man, but it is the purpose of the LORD that will stand.
zh: 人心裡的謀算很多， 唯有耶和華的計劃能實現。

en: What is desired in a man is steadfast love, and a poor man is better than a liar.
zh: 人所渴望的，就是忠誠； 作窮人比作撒謊的人還好。

en: The fear of the LORD leads to life, and whoever has it rests satisfied; he will not be visited by harm.
zh: 敬畏耶和華的，得著生命； 他必安居知足，不會遭受禍患。

en: The sluggard buries his hand in the dish and will not even bring it back to his mouth.
zh: 懶惰人把手放在盤子裡， 連拿食物送回口邊，也不願意。

en: Strike a scoffer, and the simple will learn prudence; reprove a man of understanding, and he will gain knowledge.
zh: 責打好譏笑人的，愚蒙人就會變得精明； 責備聰明人，他就會明白知識。

en: He who does violence to his father and chases away his mother is a son who brings shame and reproach.
zh: 虐待父親，趕走母親的， 是貽羞可恥的兒子。

en: Cease to hear instruction, my son, and you will stray from the words of knowledge.
zh: 我兒，你若是停止聽受管教， 就會偏離知識的言語。

en: A worthless witness mocks at justice, and the mouth of the wicked devours iniquity.
zh: 無賴作見證，嘲笑公平， 惡人的口，吞吃罪孽。

en: Condemnation is ready for scoffers, and beating for the backs of fools.
zh: 刑罰是為好譏笑人的預備的， 鞭打是為愚昧人的背預備的。
